subject: Re: can't import tilt series from IMOD
body:
Hi Juha

I got stuck at the same point, but I found out that if I manually edit the 
tomostar files to remove the decimal numbers in the tilt angle and dose 
columns, then WARP can read the tomostar files.

For example, this file is ignored (I just copied the first rows as an 
example)

data_

loop_
_wrpMovieName #1
_wrpAngleTilt #2
_wrpAxisAngle #3
_wrpAxisOffsetX #4
_wrpAxisOffsetY #5
_wrpDose #6
  TS_01_038_-57.0.mrc   57.09  85.01966    35.42966   113.2249  115
  TS_01_035_-54.0.mrc   54.11  85.04208     20.4199   93.81467  106
  TS_01_034_-51.0.mrc   51.13   85.0446    30.81794   117.7421  103
....


But this file is properly read by WARP


data_

loop_
_wrpMovieName #1
_wrpAngleTilt #2
_wrpAxisAngle #3
_wrpAxisOffsetX #4
_wrpAxisOffsetY 
_wrpDose #6
  TS_01_038_-57.0.mrc   57  85.01966    35.42966   113.2249  115
  TS_01_035_-54.0.mrc   54  85.04208     20.4199   93.81467  106
  TS_01_034_-51.0.mrc   51   85.0446    30.81794   117.7421  103
...

However, the reconstructed tomograms still do not look good, as it seems 
that the tilt angles are not taken into account for the reconstruction.

This is a more detailed tutorial for processing the EMPIAR-10164 dataset 
made by Alister Burt and Lorenzo Gaifas from the Gutsche lab (I did not try 
it out yet, though)
https://teamtomo.org/walkthroughs/EMPIAR-10164/introduction.html

Best

Genis

On Friday, February 26, 2021 at 9:08:24 AM UTC+1 juha.hu...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi All,
>
> I have been able to follow the Warp tutorial to the point where the 
> tomostar files are created based on IMOD alignment:
>
> -  I import the tilt series and alignment information to Warp
>
> - I see the tick box appearing, indicating that alignment was found from 
> the IMOD folders.
>
> - The Tomostar files are created and they point to raw frames that are in 
> the same directory.  
>
> However, nothing happens when I click start processing (the circle 
> indicating the number of items to process does not appear).
>
> Any idea why this could happen and what should I do next?
>
> best wishes,
>
> Juha
>
